item.title: SSH update uses StrictHostKeyChecking=no — remote identity not verified, MITM possible
item.intent: clawhq deploy update must verify the remote host's identity before executing commands — StrictHostKeyChecking=no accepts any host key, making the update channel vulnerable to MITM on the network path.
item.description: src/cloud/provisioning/update.ts line 143 passes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no and -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null to ssh. This disables host key verification entirely — any host that responds on instance.ipAddress will be trusted. Since ClawHQ generates the SSH keypair and injects the public key via cloud-init, the host public key is also available at provision time via the provider API (DigitalOcean, Hetzner, AWS, GCP all surface console output or host keys). Fix: during provisioning, record the SSH host public key fingerprint in the instance registry (sshHostKey field on ProvisionedInstance). In executeSsh(), write a temporary known_hosts file with that fingerprint and pass -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes -o UserKnownHostsFile=<tmpfile> instead. If fingerprint is unavailable in registry, fall back to accept-new (not no) and log a warning. Scope: engine.ts (capture host key at provision time), types.ts (sshHostKey field on ProvisionedInstance), update.ts (use known_hosts file).
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/types.ts — add optional sshHostKey?: string field to ProvisionedInstance interface

Read src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts — after createVm() returns an IP, query the provider adapter for the SSH host key fingerprint using adapter.getVmStatus() or a new method; if available, pass it to addInstance()
Read src/cloud/provisioning/registry.ts addInstance() — include sshHostKey in the instance record if provided

Read src/cloud/provisioning/update.ts executeSsh() — replace StrictHostKeyChecking=no with: if sshHostKey is present in instance record, write a temp known_hosts file and pass -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes -o UserKnownHostsFile=<tmpfile>; if absent, use -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new and log a warning
Delete the temp known_hosts file after SSH command completes (success or failure)
Run update tests — confirm StrictHostKeyChecking=no is no longer passed in any SSH invocation
acceptanceCriteria: When an instance has sshHostKey in its registry record, executeSsh() uses StrictHostKeyChecking=yes with a temp known_hosts file
When sshHostKey is absent, executeSsh() uses StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new (not no) — first-connect behavior, not blind trust
StrictHostKeyChecking=no does not appear in any SSH invocation in update.ts
